pages.full_text: 5ft high? made of ivory + hippo teeth
photo_captions: 5ft high? made of ivory + hippo teeth
pages.notes: Page contains two glued-in postcards/photos of religious art, likely from an Italian cathedral (possibly Orvieto based on the ivory reliquary/altar subject matter). Top photo shows an ornate Gothic ivory and gold altarpiece or reliquary; handwritten caption beside it reads '5ft high? made of ivory + hippo teeth'. Bottom photo shows a religious painting of the Trinity/Coronation of the Virgin. No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner. Partial text visible at right gutter edge ('w N (b...' and 'The Phi...') belongs to the right page and is not transcribed here.

pages.full_text:
We shopped. Well Simon shopped.
We camped after a long, horrible, 4 hour journey (flat crowded motorways) at 'Pian' del Voglio, a lovely rural campsite downhill from the village. This time the campsite owner yet again had a foreign wife? Philippino? Super spot. At Relax campsite (vg)
pages.notes: Upper portion of page has a decorative Italian food/bakery wrapper paper glued in, printed with 'DOLCE SALATO' repeated in teal/blue ink with food illustrations (tart, pizza, cake, croissant, muffin etc.) — this is printed ephemera, not handwritten text. Diary text continues off the bottom of the scan; the transcription is incomplete. The place name 'Pian' del Voglio' is spelled exactly as written with an apostrophe. 'Philippino' is spelled as written. Year cannot be determined from this page alone; the content (Italian motorway journey, named campsite) is consistent with the volume range 2003–2019 but no year is stated.
